title: RNA extraction protocol (Trizol)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4jpbr","text":"This protocol describes how to extract total RNA from flatworms. It is from:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fkeu3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fhj8i","text":"Hebert, F, O; Grambauer, S; Barber, I; Landry, C, R; Aubin-Horth, N (2016): Reference transcriptome sequence resource for the study of the Cestode Schistocephalus solidus, a threespine stickleback parasite. GigaScience Database. http://dx.doi.org/10.5524/100197","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":261}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":229,"length":32}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"EB7CA873C9CC4E33B4BCE5FB378E4E1D","url":"http://dx.doi.org/10.5524/100197"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"ckreu","text":"Pre-heat incubator at 65°C, clean bench surface and all the lab tools that will be used in the protocol (pipettes, tube holders, dissection forceps) with RNAse Zap/Rnase Wipes. Make sure that isopropanol + ethanol are at -20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"4ppfa","text":"Make sure to work under the fume hood for the Phenol and Chloroform extraction sections.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 1000 µL of Trizol per 50-100 mg of parsite tissues in a 2 mL eppendorf tube (RNAse free) and place the parasite tissues into their respective, labelled tube If a worm weights more than 100 mg, divide the whole worm into fractions of ~50-100 mg
Add 1 metal bead into each tube and place the tubes into a TissueLyzer. Run it at 30 Hz for 3 minutes 00:03:00
Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C 00:10:00
Discard top layer of liquid formed in the eppendorf after the centrifugation (contains fatty acids)
Transfer supernatant into a new and labelled eppendorf tube (2 mL).
Incubate at room temperature for 5 minutes. 00:05:00
Add 200 µL of chloroform per tube
Mix vigorously by inverting the tubes up and down for 1 minute. DO NOT VORTEX! 00:01:00
Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 15 minutes at 4°C 00:15:00
Transfer with precaution the aqueous phase (supernatant) into a new 2 mL eppendorf tube Transfer small volumes (ex. ~7 x 100 µL)
Add 500 µL of RNAse free isopropanol (100%) and mix thoroughly
Incubate for 15 minutes at room temperature 00:15:00 If yields are too low, incubate for 30-40 minutes
Centrifuge at 12,000 x g for 10 minutes at 4°C 00:10:00
Discard supernatant
Add 1000 µL of 75% ethanol (kept cold at -20°C) per tube
Vortex thoroughly and centrifuge at 7500g for 5 minutes at 4°C. 00:05:00
Discard supernatant and let the tubes dry out with the cap opened for 5-10 minutes 00:05:00
Add 20-50 µL of DEPC treated water into each tube
Place the tubes into the incubator (65°C) for 1 minute 00:01:00
Vortex thoroughly and repeat step 19 until RNA is completely dissolved.
